LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT PAUL STEINER'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE PLAINTIFF DID NOT MEET HIS OBLIGATIONS UNDER THE DISCOVERY ACT IN THIS CASE. FIRST, HE DID NOT RESPOND TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY. AFTER A MOTION TO COMPEL WAS FILED, HE SERVED DEFICIENT RESPONSES. AN ORDER FROM THIS COURT REQUIRED HIM TO PROVIDE ADDITIONAL RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION. THE PLAINTIFF SERVED ADDITIONAL RESPONSES WHICH DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER. SIMILARLY, THE PLAINTIFF DID NOT APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION AS ORIGINALLY NOTICED. HOWEVER, RECENTLY THE PLAINTIFF RETAINED COUNSEL WHO APPEARS TO BE MAKING STRIDES TO CURE THE PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY FAILINGS. THE PLAINTIFF SAT FOR PART OF HIS DEPOSITION RECENTLY AND THE PARTIES HAVE SCHEDULED ADDITIONAL TIME TO COMPLETE THAT DEPOSITION. THE PLAINTIFF (VERY DELINQUENTLY PRODUCED OVER 1,000 PAGES AT THE DEPOSITION. WHILE THE PLAINTIFF'S PRO PER STATUS DOES NOT EXCUSE HIS PRIOR MISDEEDS, IT APPEARS PREMATURE FOR THE EXTRAORDINARY RELIEF OF TERMINATING SANCTIONS. MONETARY SANCTION WAS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,800.00 TO BE PAID WITHIN 90 DAYS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/AJR

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL M. SIMS, ESQ., Tel No. 510-848-6601 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. CAITLIN T. DIMAGGIO, ESQ. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, Tel No. 415-788-1900 appearing for Defendant Paul Steiner.

MAtter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF RASH GHOSH'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Summary Judgment, Judge Goldsmith is retired and is not available to decide this motion. Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration of the order granting defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Per Code of Civil Procedure1008, the party seeking reconsideration must provide new evidence and a satisfactory explanation for the failure to produce that evidence at an earlier time. (Mink v. Superior Court (1992) 2 Cal.App.4th 1338; Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 689.) Plaintiff's moving papers do not explain why the evidence, attached as exhibits to his declaration, is newly discovered and why the evidence was not presented previously. Moreover, because all of the exhibits attached to plaintiff's declaration are from 2009-2012, it seems unlikely that those exhibits were newly discovered. Since plaintiff failed to comply with the requirements of section CCP 1008, the motion must be denied. Even if plaintiff had met the requirements of section 1008, he has failed to demonstrate that the evidence he presented in this motion would enable him to meet his burden on the motion for summary judgment. Defendant's motion for sanctions is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Oct-01-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL M. SIMS, TEL. NO. (510)848-6601 APPERING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JASON E. FELLNER OF MURPHY, PEARSON BRADLEY & FEENEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-788-1900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT PAUL STEINER's Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanction - Defendant's Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanctions is denied without prejudice. The plaintiff did not meet his obligations under the Discovery Act in this case. First, he did not respond to written discovery. After a motion to compel was filed, he served deficient responses. An Order from this Court required him to provide additional responses without objection. The plaintiff served additional responses which did not comply with the Court's Order. Similarly, the plaintiff did not appear for deposition as originally noticed. However, recently the plaintiff retained counsel who appears to be making strides to cure the plaintiff's discovery failings. The plaintiff sat for part of his deposition recently and the parties have scheduled additional time to complete that deposition. The plaintiff (very delinquently produced over 1,000 pages at the deposition. While the plaintiff's pro per status does not excuse his prior misdeeds, it appears premature for the extraordinary relief of terminating sanctions. Monetary sanction was granted in the amount of $2,800.00 to be paid within 90 days. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr
